title: phoradendron racemosum
intro: Phoradendron racemosum, also known as Eastern mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on various tree species, particularly oak and pine trees. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which are attributed to the presence of iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Phoradendron racemosum are said to include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Phoradendron racemosum can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
